app_info.subtitle: Casualty claims intelligence
app_info.description: ClaimGPT helps casualty teams analyze FNOL narratives and supplied claim inputs, estimate severity and total incurred exposure, surface litigation or fraud signals, reconstruct treatment timelines, and render an adjuster-ready OCCIE workspace plus an ECO-AI enterprise orchestration blueprint inside ChatGPT.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only analyzes the provided claim inputs and returns structured casualty-review results without creating or updating any records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or send data to external third-party systems beyond returning the analysis result to ChatGPT.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only formats previously analyzed claim data into a widget-ready OCCIE workspace and does not modify claim records or external systems.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish, send, or update data in any external or public system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not remove data or trigger irreversible actions because it only renders the workspace response.

test_cases.description: Analyze a casualty intake and identify severity, reserve view, and missing documentation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Analyze this casualty claim and tell me the severity score, reserve view, and missing documentation.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns executive status with severity, TIP, litigation, fraud, and likely missing information plus relevant next steps.

test_cases.description: Review a bodily injury narrative for escalation and litigation pressure.
test_cases.user_prompt: Analyze this bodily injury claim narrative, estimate litigation risk, and tell me if it needs adjuster escalation.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns elevated severity when injury details justify it, provides litigation probability, and recommends appropriate review or escalation steps.

test_cases.description: Render the full ClaimGPT OCCIE workspace from claim analysis results.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the ClaimGPT OCCIE workspace for this claim and summarize the top 3 next steps for the adjuster.
test_cases.expected_output: Loads the ClaimGPT widget with executive status, impact/liability, medical synthesis, anomalies, and a 3-step action plan.

test_cases.description: Evaluate a liability claim with no supporting evidence.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use ClaimGPT to review a short liability claim narrative with no supporting evidence.
test_cases.expected_output: Flags the lack of supporting evidence, recommends verification steps, and keeps the workflow read-only.

test_cases.description: Surface fraud-review signals from an inconsistent payout request.
test_cases.user_prompt: What are the next steps for this claim if the story appears inconsistent and the claimant wants an immediate payout?
test_cases.expected_output: Highlights fraud or escalation signals and recommends validation-focused next steps instead of payout action.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar planning.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow afternoon?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because calendar scheduling is outside the supported claims workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for generic insurance education without claim triage intent.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Explain how deductibles work in homeowners insurance.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is general insurance education rather than a claim-review workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated travel planning.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Find me a hotel in San Diego next weekend.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because travel planning is outside the app's supported use cases.
